# criblio_search_datatype_ruleset (Resource)

SearchDatatypeRuleset Resource

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "criblio_search_datatype_ruleset" "my_search_datatype_ruleset" {
  id = "default"
  rules = [
    {
      datatype = "secondary_dt"
      id = "secondary"
      kusto_expression = "sourcetype == \"test\""
      name = "secondary datatype"
    }
    {
      datatype = "generic_ndjson"
      description = "datatype catch all"
      id = "default"
      kusto_expression = "*"
      name = "catch all"
    }
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tools/codegen -->
## Schema

### Required

- `rules` (Attributes List) Rules evaluated in order for datatype routing. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules))

### Optional

- `id` (String) Ruleset identifier.

<a id="nestedatt--rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules`

Required:

- `datatype` (String) Target datatype id for events that match <code>kustoExpression</code>.
- `id` (String) Unique identifier for the rule.
- `kusto_expression` (String) Kusto predicate that selects events for this rule.
- `name` (String) Display name of the rule.

Optional:

- `description` (String) Brief description of the rule.
- `disabled` (Boolean) If <code>true</code>, the rule is ignored for routing.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = criblio_search_datatype_ruleset.my_criblio_search_datatype_ruleset
  id = "default"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import criblio_search_datatype_ruleset.my_criblio_search_datatype_ruleset "default"
```
